title: Scabiosa asterusica
intro: Scabiosa asterusica, also known as Piney Ground Pine, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, belonging to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). Traditionally, Scabiosa asterusica has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Scabiosa asterusica is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into medicinal baths and topical creams to alleviate skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Scabiosa asterusica is a member of the plant family Dipsacaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as teasels or scabious. This family is part of the larger order Dipsacales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as Caprifoliaceae and Adoxaceae. The Dipsacaceae family is characterized by its distinctive inflorescences and the presence of five-merous flowers. Within the Dipsacaceae family, Scabiosa asterusica is classified as a species within the genus Scabiosa, which comprises a number of species commonly known as scabious. The classification of Scabiosa asterusica reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the plant family Dipsacaceae.
